fields.dosage_form: lyophilized powder for solution, for intravenous use
fields.manufacturer_labeler: Wyeth BioPharma Division of Wyeth Pharmaceuticals LLC
fields.indication_summary: Recombinant human blood coagulation factor IX indicated for adults and children with hemophilia B (congenital factor IX deficiency or Christmas disease) for on-demand treatment and control of bleeding episodes, perioperative management of bleeding, and routine prophylaxis to reduce the frequency of bleeding episodes. Not indicated for induction of immune tolerance.
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Lyophilized powder in single-use vials containing nominally 250, 500, 1000, 2000, or 3000 IU

notes: Biologic licensed under a BLA (BLA103677), like PREVNAR 20 and unlike the small-molecule NDA labels in this set. Brand name is styled 'BeneFIX' (mixed case) in the label itself, though the SPL title uppercases it -- exact-match evaluation should be case-insensitive for this document. Has two distinct, both-legitimate 'Revised:' dates: Prescribing Information says 11/2022, SPL trailing block says 2/2025. Dosing is weight- and recovery-based (IU/kg formula), structurally different from the fixed-dose small-molecule labels. Five vial strengths each carry their own vial NDC (58394-133/134/135/136/137-03) plus a parallel kit NDC series (58394-633 through -637); no single ndc value can represent that, so left null rather than picking one arbitrarily.
